calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. YUKI HARAGUCHI, Esq. of Holmes Pittman & Haraguchi, LLP (tel: 202-660-2788) for Petitioner
Atty. DAVID ZOLKIN, Esq. of Weintraub Zolkin Talerico & Selth LLP (tel: 424-500-8552) for YER ABC, LLC as assignee of WYRE Payments, Inc.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER AYOUB BARKIA's Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Counsel Zolkin failed to contest the matter in a timely fashion. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner AYOUB BARKIA's petition to confirm the interim arbitration award is granted. (Hightower v. Superior Ct. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1415, 1438-39.) Confirmation is necessary to effectuate the arbitrator's choice of remedy. Yer ABC, LLC, the alleged assignee of defendants, argues that the court should deny this motion because it is liquidating defendants' assets and confirmation of the arbitrator's security award would conflict with governmental restrictions. Yer ABC, LLC's only proof of these restrictions is a naked declaration from one of its members. (Yorkison Decl.) There is nothing from the entities themselves (FDIC/DFPI/CFTC). In addition, Yer ABC LLC presents no basis to vacate or correct the award in compliance with CCP 1286.2 or CCP 1286.6.

The petition is granted and petitioner may seek a judgment thereon. Since Yer ABC, LLC voluntarily appeared in this action and is defendants' alleged assignee, it may be named on the judgment too.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Continued To Apr-08-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. YUKI HARAGUCHI, Esq. of Holmes Pittman & Haraguchi, LLP (tel: 202-660-2788) for Petitioner.
Atty. KATHERINE LIU, Esq. of Weintraub Zolkin Talerico & Selth LLP (tel: 424-500-8552) for Respondent.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER AYOUB BARKIA's Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

Matter discussed. The Court holds a discussion regading the late opposition filed by Respondent. Counsel Liu states Respondent was not properly served. Counsel Haraguchi is opposed to the Court considering the late opposition. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court orders as follows: Respondent is to file an opposition by March 21, 2024. Plaintiff is to file a reply by March 28, 2024. The Court continues the matter to April 8, 2024 at 9:30 A.M. in Department 302.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
